title: Bequest
description: A disposition of property or money in a will. Supports 7 core types (specific, pecuniary, demonstrative, general, residuary, life_interest, class) and extension types via extensionType (e.g. wasiyya, halachic_matanah).

BeneficiaryOrganisation.description: An organisation that receives a bequest — charity, company, trust, or unincorporated association.

type.$comment: charity: registered charity. company: limited company or corporation. unincorporated_association: club, society, or group without legal personality. trust: a trust entity. other: any organisation type not covered above.
type.description: The legal form of the organisation.

registrationNumber.description: Charity registration number, company number, or other official registration.

jurisdiction.description: Jurisdiction where the organisation is registered.

LifeInterest.description: Details of a life interest — one person enjoys the asset during their lifetime, then it passes to the remainderman.

tenantForLifeId.description: Reference to the Person.id who holds the life interest.

remaindermanId.description: Reference to the Person.id who receives the asset after the life interest ends.

remaindermanClassDefinition.description: Natural-language description of the remainderman class if not a named individual.
examples: All my grandchildren living at the death of the life tenant, in equal shares

interestType.$comment: use_and_income: right to use the asset and receive any income it generates. income_only: right to income only, not physical use. use_only: right to occupy or use, but no income entitlement. protective: a protective trust — converts to discretionary trust if the life tenant attempts to assign their interest. right_of_residence: right to live in the property for life.
interestType.description: The scope of the life interest.

Substitution.description: A substitute beneficiary who receives the bequest if the primary beneficiary cannot (e.g. predeceases the testator).

beneficiaryId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the substitute beneficiary.

classDefinition.description: Natural-language description of the substitute class.
examples: The children of the deceased beneficiary, in equal shares

beneficiaryOrganisation.description: Substitute organisation if the substitution is to a charity or company.

condition.description: The condition that triggers this substitution.
examples: If the primary beneficiary disclaims the gift

perStirpes.description: Whether the substitution is per stirpes — passing to the deceased beneficiary's descendants by representation.

PostDeathAction.description: An action taken after death that modifies the bequest — disclaimer, deed of variation, appropriation, or assent.

type.$comment: disclaimer: beneficiary formally refuses the gift. deed_of_variation: beneficiary redirects the gift to another person (within 2 years of death in the UK for IHT purposes). appropriation: executor transfers a specific asset to satisfy the bequest. assent: executor formally vests the asset in the beneficiary.

examples: Deed of variation redirecting £50,000 to grandchildren's trust
Beneficiary disclaimed life interest in favour of children

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity recording this action.

InheritanceResponse.description: The beneficiary's response to the inheritance — acceptance, renunciation, or qualified acceptance. In civil law jurisdictions, acceptance may be required within a time limit.

response.$comment: accepted: beneficiary has accepted the inheritance. renounced: beneficiary has formally renounced — the gift lapses or passes to a substitute. qualified_acceptance: beneficiary accepted with limitations (e.g. accepting only up to the value of assets, not liabilities — common in Japan and France). pending: no response yet — may be within the statutory response period.
response.description: The beneficiary's formal response to the inheritance.

responsePersonId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the respondent.

courtFiled.description: Whether the response was filed with a court (required in some jurisdictions).

deadlineDate.description: Statutory deadline for responding (e.g. 3 months in Japan, 4 months in France).

id.description: Unique identifier for this bequest within the INHERIT document.

bequestType.$comment: specific: a named item to a named person (e.g. 'my engagement ring to my daughter'). pecuniary: a fixed cash sum (e.g. '£10,000 to my son'). demonstrative: a cash sum from a specified fund (e.g. '£5,000 from my Barclays savings account'). general: from the general estate, not a specific item. residuary: the remainder after all other gifts and debts are paid. life_interest: right to use or benefit during lifetime, then passes to a remainderman. class: to a category of people (e.g. 'my grandchildren equally').

extensionType.$comment: Non-core bequest types (e.g. wasiyya, halachic_matanah). When set, extension schemas handle validation.
extensionType.description: Extension-specific bequest type not covered by the core enum. Validated by the relevant extension schema.

beneficiaryId.description: Reference to the Person.id of the individual beneficiary.

beneficiaryOrganisation.description: Organisation receiving this bequest, if not an individual.

classDefinition.$comment: Natural-language description of the class of beneficiaries (e.g. 'All my grandchildren living at my death').
classDefinition.description: Natural-language definition of the beneficiary class. Required for class bequests. Must be precise enough for executors to identify all members.
examples: All my grandchildren living at my death
The children of my late brother Ahmed
The descendants of my sister Lakshmi
The children of my late uncle Kwame

lifeInterest.description: Life interest details, required when type is 'life_interest'.

amount.description: Cash amount for pecuniary and demonstrative bequests, in minor currency units.

sharePercentage.description: Percentage share of the residuary estate or a specified fund.

description.description: Free-text description of the bequest — what is being given.
examples: My engagement ring and matching necklace
The entire contents of my wine cellar
All my shares in Acme Ltd

sourceAssetId.$comment: For demonstrative bequests — the asset from which the amount should be paid.
sourceAssetId.description: Reference to the Asset.id from which a demonstrative bequest should be paid.

conditions.$comment: Conditions that must be met before the bequest takes effect.
conditions.description: Conditions precedent that must be satisfied before the beneficiary receives this bequest.
examples: Beneficiary must have attained the age of 25
Beneficiary must be married at the date of my death

substitutions.description: Substitute beneficiaries in case the primary beneficiary cannot receive the gift.

hotchpot.$comment: Whether lifetime gifts to the beneficiary should be brought into account when calculating their share.
hotchpot.description: Whether lifetime gifts (advances) to the beneficiary are brought into account when calculating their share. Common in intestacy and some wills.

distributionMethod.$comment: per_capita: each beneficiary receives an equal share. per_stirpes: shares pass by representation — a predeceased beneficiary's share goes to their descendants. modified_per_stirpes: like per_stirpes but sharing begins at the first generation with living members. per_capita_at_each_generation: shares at each generation divided equally among living members and descendants of deceased members. halachic_yerusha: Jewish inheritance rules (firstborn double share, patrilineal only).
distributionMethod.description: How the bequest is distributed among multiple beneficiaries.

predeceaseRule.$comment: lapse: gift fails if the beneficiary predeceases the testator — falls into the residuary estate. per_stirpes: passes to the predeceased beneficiary's issue by representation. substitution: passes to a named substitute beneficiary. accrual: redistributed among surviving co-beneficiaries of the same gift. statutory_default: the jurisdiction's default rule applies (e.g. s.33 Wills Act 1837 in England and Wales).
predeceaseRule.description: What happens if the beneficiary dies before the testator.

constrainedBy.$comment: testamentary_freedom: testator may dispose of this gift freely. customary_rule: customary law constrains or overrides this bequest. forced_heirship: civil law forced heirship rules constrain this bequest. religious_rule: religious law constrains this bequest (e.g. wasiyya limited to one-third). coparcenary_survivorship: Hindu coparcenary property passes by survivorship, not by will.
constrainedBy.description: Which legal principle constrains this bequest, if any.

customaryOverride.$comment: Whether customary/religious law overrides testamentary freedom for this bequest.
customaryOverride.description: Whether customary or religious law overrides the testator's freedom to dispose of this gift as they wish.

postDeathActions.description: Post-death actions that modified this bequest — disclaimers, deeds of variation, appropriations.

inheritanceResponse.description: The beneficiary's formal response to this inheritance, if applicable.

notes.description: Free-text notes about this bequest. Use for context, testator intent, or special instructions to executors.
examples: This ring has been in the family for four generations — please ensure it stays in the family
If the charity has merged or changed name, the executor should identify the successor organisation

allOf.$comment: Core types (specific, pecuniary, demonstrative, general, residuary) require beneficiaryId OR beneficiaryOrganisation. When extensionType is set, extension schema handles validation.
Life interest bequests require lifeInterest object.
